action.hypothesis: A low-rank trunk plus group-local winner-take-all residual routing can improve support diversity and regularity at low K, making `K=32` more competitive at the mandated small-capacity setting `EF=8` than the existing low-rank gated residual path.
action.summary: Implemented a new `lowrank_grouped_residual` family under `sparsify/`. It reuses the low-rank dense trunk, then applies group-wise local competition before the global `K` selection on the residual branch, targeting better low-K utilization and a more deployment-friendly access pattern. Registered the architecture in config validation and class dispatch. A local instantiate/forward/backward sanity check passed for `architecture=lowrank_grouped_residual`, `EF=8`, `K=32`.
action.self_review: This is a clean one-variable architecture probe aligned with the stagnation policy. Main risk is that the fixed default `group_topk_size=4` may be suboptimal, but the implementation is minimal, compatible with the existing execution layer, and should produce an interpretable first signal.
notes_to_memory: Round 59+ showed severe stagnation and multiple Ks already explored, so this round pivots to a fundamentally different architecture instead of another local sweep.
Added `lowrank_grouped_residual`: low-rank trunk plus grouped residual competition, intended to improve feature diversity and routing regularity specifically for low-K runs.
Local forward/backward sanity passed for `lowrank_grouped_residual` at `EF=8`, `K=32`; if training fails, treat it as an implementation issue rather than an architectural verdict.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_grouped_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=8` shows promising proxy quality or runtime regularity, keep the family fixed and sweep `K` upward slightly (for example `K=40`) before touching EF.
If this grouped residual prototype is clearly weak at `K=32`, `EF=8`, try another fundamentally different low-K mechanism rather than returning to minor tuning on the current gated mainline.
